text: the few of us that go out don’t get enough. The UV lamps don’t cut it, and they won’t last forever. The elderly are brittle, and too many of the young show signs of rickets .” “Will your people fight, Miss Brooks? Will they lay down their lives for a chance at being free of Warlord?” Rodriguez asked . “I can’t speak for them, but I think if they’re given a chance, if they’re given just a little hope...they would. They have nothing else .” “And the bots?” Rodriguez’s eyes flickered to Ronin . “I don’t know. Most are what you’d consider civilians, and I cannot guess the nature of their programming. Warlord’s lackeys will fight for him, but beyond that, I don’t know with any degree of certainty. Though …” Ronin shifted his optics to Newton. The first voice Ronin heard after reactivation, the source of his oldest uncorrupted memory... “If anyone could convince them, it would be the Prophet .” The plates over Newton’s optics rose high as the room’s attention fell upon him. “The stories told about me as the Prophet are exaggerated,” he said, a faint shakiness in his voice. “It borders on superstitious nonsense — ” “You’re the one who woke them up,” William said. “As bots have developed emotions over the years, that’s only become more significant to them. Stories are powerful things, whether we’re flesh or metal .” “But to think that I’d have any special sway over anyone else...I don’t — ” “Brooks, are you willing to speak to your people? To rally as many to the fight as you can?” Rodriguez asked . “I’ll do what I can,” Lara said . He nodded and turned to Newton. “She’s going to risk everything, again, because she thinks this is the right thing to do. What about you? Will you talk to the bots in Cheyenne ?” Newton frowned. “I...yes. Yes, I will. Illogical as it may be to walk into the den of our enemy, stand up in the middle of the square, and incite a riot, I will .” “Lara is still wounded,” Ronin said, “and any gearhead that’s seen her would shoot on sight. She can’t go back to Cheyenne .” Lara turned to him. “I need to do this, Ronin .” “Anyone else can do it,” he replied. “I need you safe. Alive .” She pulled her hand free and cupped his jaw. “I can’t ask them to sacrifice and not be willing to do the same. I know the people there, and they know me.” Leaning forward, she pressed her forehead to his. “We can’t let Warlord go on like this .” His processors calculated hundreds, thousands, millions of possibilities. Countless ways in which it could all go wrong, countless ways for him to lose her again. Permanently. But he knew her well enough to know he wouldn’t win this fight. He took her face in his hands and kissed her, shutting down the calculations, pushing aside his worry . “She’s to be as far away from any fighting as possible,” Ronin said after he pulled away, turning to Rodriguez . The Colonel nodded. “We’ll make a plan based on the map you drew for us. For now, I’m sure we’re all fed up with this meeting. We’ll reconvene tomorrow morning to begin preparations.” Rodriguez raised a hand, silencing Dave Elliot before the man could get out a word. “ Dismissed .” Chapter Thirty - One L ara squeezed the trigger. The pistol fired, and her arms jerked, but she kept her hold and leveled the barrel quickly. The gunshot echoed through the chamber, and her ears rang even with the earplugs Ronin had given her . He tipped his head forward, chin brushing her shoulder. His solid presence at her back was a big help in controlling the gun, but it was as much a matter of knowing what to expect. She’d learned a lot since she shot at him back in Cheyenne . Not that her aim had improved much . “One foot, two inches to the left and ten inches down from your mark.” Ronin’s voice was clear through the earplugs, thanks to the tiny electronics he said were inside them. That he could figure the distance so accurately was as amazing as it was discouraging . She glared at him over her shoulder . “It’s a little closer than before,” he added, smirking . “By what? An inch ?” “Five-eighths of an inch, actually .” Lara rolled her eyes and faced forward. Her target was a small circle drawn on a wooden plank, twenty feet away. She couldn’t tell any of her shots from the other holes, but she trusted Ronin . Taking a deep breath, she aimed the pistol and pulled the trigger. It boomed again. Wood splintered at the top left corner of the board . “ Closer . Only — ” “What is the point?” Lara asked, flicking on the safety and lowering her arms. “What kind of damage can this do ?” “None, if you don’t start improving your aim .” She turned toward him, narrowing her eyes. “ Ronin .” “It depends what you’re shooting at .” “You know damn well I’d be shooting at a bot .” “Not all bots are built like me.” He banged his knuckles on his chest. The solid thud was muted by his clothes, but she knew well what was under his skin. “That pistol would barely put a scratch in my casing. But not all of Warlord’s gearheads are outfitted for war .” “How much use am I going to be? I’m not a soldier, Ronin. Shouldn’t I leave the shooting to them ?” “ You are the one who insists on going. You know what use you will be, and if it goes as planned, you won’t have to fire a single shot. But I won’t have you there, away from me, without some way to defend yourself .” “There will
